query_normalized: detecting distribution shift in public health surveillance data via kernel two‑sample tests statistics
rationale: The paper studies kernel‑based two‑sample (MMD) tests and their optimality for detecting distribution differences, which matches the mechanism (kernel two‑sample statistics) of the user's question, serving as a foundational methodological reference even though it does not focus on public‑health data. This satisfies acceptance criterion (a) and (d).
